---
title: "scPanel"
description: "Automatic discovery of sparse, generalizable gene panels for patient classification from scRNA-seq."
date: 2024-09-01
image: scpanel.png
categories: [single-cell, biomarker discovery, machine learning, Python]
---

**scPanel** is an open-source Python tool that automatically identifies **sparse gene
panels** for generalizable patient classification from single-cell RNA-seq data.

[{{< fa brands github >}} Code](https://github.com/carissaxie/scPanel){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
[{{< fa book >}} Paper](https://doi.org/10.1093/bib/bbae482){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
[{{< fa file-lines >}} Preprint](https://doi.org/10.1101/2024.04.09.588647){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}

## The problem
Translating single-cell discoveries into clinically usable biomarkers is hard: signal is
spread across thousands of genes and many cell types, and panels that look good on one
dataset often fail to generalize to another.

## The approach
scPanel addresses this in two stages:

1. **Find the responsive cells.** It identifies the cell population(s) most responsive to
   the perturbation of interest (e.g. a disease or drug), so panel selection focuses where
   the signal actually is.
2. **Select a sparse panel.** It then uses a data-driven procedure to automatically
   determine *how many* genes are needed and *which* ones, yielding a minimal panel for
   patient classification — implemented with parallel computing for scalability.

## Key result
On scleroderma and COVID-19 datasets, scPanel achieved high patient-classification
accuracy using **fewer than 20 automatically selected genes**, and reached **100%
cross-dataset accuracy** predicting COVID-19 disease state on an external dataset —
demonstrating that the selected genes generalize.

## Reference
Yi Xie, Jianfei Yang, John F. Ouyang, Enrico Petretto.
*scPanel: a tool for automatic identification of sparse gene panels for generalizable
patient classification using scRNA-seq datasets.*
**Briefings in Bioinformatics** 25(6), 2024, bbae482.
[doi:10.1093/bib/bbae482](https://doi.org/10.1093/bib/bbae482)
